channel.title: Switzerland on Cédric Walter blog
channel.description: Recent content in Switzerland on Cédric Walter blog
channel.copyright: Reproduction without explicit permission is prohibited. All Rights Reserved. All photos remain copyright © their rightful owners. No copyright infringement is intended.

item.title: Alternate way to watch Init7 TV7 – Swiss Live Streaming Broadcast Television
item.description: I struggled last time with PLEX media server, you need to install xTeVe, then UDPproxy to rewrite the content of the m3u (do not exist for PI) this emulate the TVTuner, import the XML TV guide and define the tuner mapping (a pain!) add that this is not easily working on a raspberry pi… a disaster Till I Discover JellyFin, Live TV is supported out of the box! You just need to add the m3u and the XML TV guide!

item.title: Understanding Trail Difficulty Ratings: S1, S2, S3, S4, S5 Explained
item.description: Table Of Contents Trail Difficulty Rating System (TDRS) International Trail Rating System (ITRS) S0 trails? S1: Easy Trails S2: Moderate Trails S3 – Difficult Trails S4 – Very Difficult Trails S5 – Extremely Difficult Trails Choosing the Right Trail for You Embarking on a hiking or mountain biking adventure can be an exhilarating and rewarding experience. However, before you hit the trails, it’s essential to understand the trail difficulty ratings, such as S1, S2, S3, S4 and S5, to ensure your safety and enjoyment.

item.title: Lake Zürich
item.description: Lake Zürich (Swiss German/Alemannic: Zürisee; German: Zürichsee) is a lake in Switzerland, extending southeast of the city of Zürich. Depending on the context, Lake Zürich or Zürichsee can be used to describe the lake as a whole, or just that part of the lake downstream of the Seedamm at Rapperswil, whilst the part upstream of Rapperswil may be called the Obersee or Upper Lake. The Zürichsee-Schifffahrtsgesellschaft – the Lake Zürich Navigation Company – provides with its 17-passenger ships touristic services on Lake Zürich.

item.title: Grimsel Pass
item.description: The Grimsel Pass (German: Grimselpass; French: Col du Grimsel; Italian: Passo del Grimsel) is a mountain pass in Switzerland, crossing the Bernese Alps at an elevation of 2,164 metres (7,100 ft). The pass connects the Haslital, the upper valley of the river Aare, with the upper valley of the Rhône. In so doing, and as the Aare is a tributary of the Rhine, the pass crosses the continental divide between the North Sea and the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Furka Pass
item.description: Furka Pass (French: Le Col de la Furka), with an elevation of 2,429 metres (7,969 ft), is a high mountain pass in the Swiss Alps connecting Gletsch, Valais with Realp, Uri. The Furka Oberalp Bahn line through the Furka Tunnel bypasses the pass. The base tunnel opened in 1982 and replaced a tunnel at 2100 m. The Furka Pass was used as a location in the James Bond film Goldfinger. One curve on the east side of the pass is even named “James Bond Strasse”.

item.title: Susten Pass
item.description: Susten Pass (German: Sustenpass) is a mountain pass in the Swiss Alps. The pass road, built from 1938–1945, connects Innertkirchen in the canton of Bern with Wassen in the canton of Uri. A 300-metre long tunnel crosses the pass at 2,224 metres. The pass is popular with tourists, by car, motorbike or bicycles especially for the views of the Stein Glacier on the south side. There are numerous great views and a number of places to stop for photos.

item.title: Switzerland Interlaken
item.description: Jungfrau Park is an amusement park located near Interlaken, Switzerland. It opened as the Mystery Park in 2003, and closed in November 2006 due to financial difficulties and low turnout. The park was designed by Erich von Däniken, and consisted of seven pavilions, each of which explored one of several great “mysteries” of the world.

item.title: Hallwillersee in the Canton of Aargau, Switzerland
item.description: Lake Hallwil is a lake largely in the Canton of Aargau, Switzerland, located at 47°17′N 8°12′E. It is the largest lake in Aargau and lies mostly in the districts of Lenzburg and Kulm on the southern edge of the canton. The southern tip of the lake reaches into the canton of Lucerne. Hallwilersee Hallwilersee Hallwilersee Hallwilersee Hallwilersee Hallwilersee

item.title: La Tour-de-Peilz
item.description: La Tour-de-Peilz is a municipality in Riviera-Pays-d ‘Enhaut District in the canton of Vaud in Switzerland. The city is located on Lake Geneva between Montreux and Vevey.

item.title: Vevey in the canton of Vaud, Switzerland
item.description: Vevey is a town in Switzerland in the canton of Vaud, on the north shore of Lake Geneva, near Lausanne. The German name Vivis is no longer commonly used. It was the seat of the district of the same name until 2006, and is now part of the Riviera-Pays-d ‘Enhaut District. It is part of the French-speaking area of Switzerland. Vevey is home to the world headquarters of the international food and beverage company Nestlé, founded here in 1867.

item.title: Walensee in St Gallen
item.description: The Walensee, also known as Lake Walen or Lake Walenstadt from Walenstadt, is one of the larger lakes in Switzerland, with about two thirds of its area in the Canton of St. Gallen and about one third in the Canton of Glarus. Other towns and villages at the lake include Weesen, Quinten, Quarten, and Murg. The three main rivers leading to the lake are the Seez, Murgbach, and Linth. The last continues its course from Walensee to Lake Zurich.

item.title: Eiger in the the Bernese Alps
item.description: The Eiger is a 3,967-metre mountain of the Bernese Alps, overlooking Grindelwald and Lauterbrunnen in the Bernese Oberland of Switzerland, just north of the main watershed and border with Valais. ## Visiting an Alphorn manufacture Alphorns are traditional Swiss folkmusic instrument
